rationale: The citing paper uses Lee and Bradlow (2011) as a key prior benchmark in its literature framing, including the argument that market structure analysis often starts with predefined attributes. It then explicitly positions P2V-MAP as extending that prior work by automating attribute discovery via a different data source and modeling pipeline. The citation supports the paper's core contribution claim (automation and scalability beyond review-text settings), not just background listing.
relation_description: The citing paper presents P2V-MAP as an extension of Lee and Bradlow's automated attribute-generation approach for market structure analysis. It preserves the automation objective but replaces review text mining with basket co-occurrence embeddings to enable broader, cross-category, retailer-scale mapping.
